title: brachiaria mutica
intro: Brachiaria mutica, also known as para grass, is a highly invasive perennial grass native to Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and acting as an antihelmintic agent. Brachiaria mutica is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can be found in various forms in traditional medicine and some local markets.
